reasoning:
The forecast relies on comparing future published values of the FRED series IC4WSA against its value as of the 2025-11-09 cutoff date. The most recent data published by cutoff corresponds to the week ending Nov 1, which the FRED data reports as 225,500. 

For the first resolution date (2025-11-16), resolving to the data for the week ending Nov 8, the FRED history tool actually retrieved the value: 227,000. Since 227,000 > 225,500, the first resolution date is extremely likely to be YES, subject only to routine subsequent revisions by the Labor Department. 

For the second resolution date (2025-12-09), looking at the week ending Nov 29, recent weekly initial claims have hovered around 229,000. As these newer, slightly higher weekly figures replace older numbers in the 4-week window, the moving average will likely stay above the 225,500 baseline. 

For the third resolution date (2026-02-07), projecting 3 months outward introduces substantial uncertainty. While the labor market is nominally cooling, early 2025 saw high volatility in jobless claims from federal layoffs, but the overall multi-month trend has recently flattened. In alignment with increasing uncertainty over time horizons, this distal date is pegged at a coin flip (0.50).
